gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is a scaffold with no substantive articles (Articles I–VI are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'). The Mission Statement Protocol is designed to translate the human-authored BoR into an operational constitution of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules. Without a defined BoR, the DIO lacks a constitutional identity and perimeter; Arbiter (#190) cannot enforce scope, and Nomos (#200) has no mission-aligned constraints to adjudicate. The organism therefore operates in an unbounded default-permissive state with no enterprise guardrails, prohibited activities, or core identity codified.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-bor-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (v1.0.0-seed) is a scaffold with all articles undefined. The DIO is operating without a human-authorised enterprise scope (Article I), prohibited activities (Article II), core identity (Article III), human authority reservation (Article IV), or interpretation rules. This means the Arbiter scope gate has no substantive perimeter, and any action – including the currently dispatched routine maintenance goals – cannot be assessed against human boundaries.",
      "source": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise), Article II (Prohibited Activities), Article III (Core Identity), Article IV (Human Authority Reservation)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "reason": "Absence of BoR content creates an unbounded enterprise. All dispatched goals (governance-class events) lack scope validation; any action would be authorised by default, risking human-interest violation and undermining the entire compliance architecture."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-msp-001",
      "description": "The MSP seed (v1.0.0-seed) contains no operational rules, yet all 10 Recent Goals reference 'MSP §routine-operations' as their mission_ref. That section does not exist in the current MSP. The system is operating under default-permissive MSP evaluation while relying on a non-existent compliance anchor for its goals.",
      "source": "MSP §Rules (empty), MSP §Genome References (empty)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "reason": "Dispatched governance-class events claim mission compliance with an undefined MSP section. This creates a false sense of alignment and may cause Nomos to miss necessary constraints. While the MSP is currently permissive, the misreference indicates a gap between operational record-keeping and the MSP’s stated structure."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "gap-state-001",
      "description": "The Governance State Slice is empty – no governance-class transitions have been recorded. With a scaffold BoR and a permissive MSP, the absence of any recorded governance events (such as BoR amendment requests or PEM-driven MSP amendment proposals) suggests that the governance feedback loop is not active, leaving no trace of how the system’s operations are being evaluated against the BoR.",
      "source": "BoR Article V (Amendment Protocol – undefined), MSP §Amendment Protocol (requires PEMs and BoR amendments for scope issues)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "reason": "Without governance events, the system cannot demonstrate that it is correctly identifying and escalating BoR or MSP compliance issues. The recent goals, which likely arose from gap detection, have no associated governance record to confirm they were cleared against an authoritative BoR perimeter, weakening audit and accountability."
    }
  ]
}

      "constitutional_attention_needed": "The MSP must either be amended to contain a 'routine-operations' section that justifies these goals, or the goal dispatcher must be adjusted to reference actual MSP sections. This gap indicates a disconnect between the organism's operational engine and its constitution."
    }
  ]
